description:
## Question

What should the sidebar Current Workspace control and its picker look and feel like across: no Current Workspace, choosing a directory through the native picker, browsing remembered Workspaces, pending selection, validation failure, load failure with retry, and removal? Create a cheap visual/interaction prototype for human review and link it from the resolution.

comments.text:
## Prototype ready for review

Three dev-only Current Workspace switcher variants are available on the existing Issue Explorer host: [workspace-switcher.prototype.tsx](src/workspace-switcher.prototype.tsx).

Open http://127.0.0.1:1420/?prototype=workspace-switcher&variant=A&scenario=ready in a development build. Use left/right arrows to compare A (inline selector), B (workspace drawer), and C (status dock); the scenario selector covers no workspace, validation, validation failure, load failure/retry, and removal. This is explicitly throwaway code, gated to development only.

## Resolution

The review selected **Variant A — Inline selector**.

The future product control belongs in the existing sidebar's Current Workspace area: show the active Workspace basename and its full path, then reveal remembered Workspaces and the native Choose folder action from that compact control. Keep the catalog adjacent to the control rather than taking over the Issue Explorer or adding a permanent workspace drawer.

The selected direction must still expose the specified states in-place: an empty chooser when none is Current, a pending validation/load state, inline validation feedback, a retryable load-failure banner while the old Issue List remains visible, unavailable remembered entries, and local-only removal. The prototype has been deleted rather than promoted; this is a design direction, not feature implementation.

title: Prototype the Current Workspace switcher
